strata.label: Meltemi wind layer
strata.note: Etesian NW winds — evaporation and cooling that shape every island's water balance.

strata.label: Island relief
strata.note: Surface topography — steep, rocky, low storage.

strata.label: Granite grus / regolith
strata.note: Granite disaggregates grain-by-grain into permeable sand — rain drains straight through.

strata.label: Schist / marble regolith
strata.note: Thin regolith over hard rock; marble karst can store and release water at springs.

strata.label: Pyroclastic tuff & lava
strata.note: Ash, pumice and lava — sometimes an aquifer, often hydrothermally altered.

strata.label: Blueschist / marble bedrock
strata.note: High-pressure/low-temperature rock — the exhumed record of a subduction interface.

strata.label: Granodiorite pluton
strata.note: A Miocene melt frozen at shallow depth; its weathering skin is the grus above.

strata.label: Volcanic pile / caldera fill
strata.note: Stacked eruptions and caldera collapse deposits.

strata.label: Aegean continental crust
strata.note: Thinned, extending continental crust of the Aegean back-arc.

strata.label: Exhumed subduction interface (HP/LT)
strata.note: 40–20 Ma: rocks buried in subduction, then exhumed — why blueschist sits at the surface today.

strata.label: Miocene granite emplacement
strata.note: 14–9 Ma: granite intrudes during Aegean extension.

strata.label: South Aegean volcanic arc (active)
strata.note: The still-active arc — Santorini, Milos, Nisyros above the modern subduction zone.
